results.title: GetCompress Review - AI Tool Features and Tips
results.content:
Last modified:  June 28, 2026  | Fred Magni

Greetings, fellow digital alchemists! Ever felt like your digital assets were staging a hostile takeover of your storage, or that optimizing them was a soul-crushing saga of context switching? Enter GetCompress: the desktop utility that’s less of a tool and more of a workflow superpower. Designed for macOS, Windows, and Linux, it’s your local, lossless media compression hub, obliterating file sizes by up to 90% without ever sacrificing your precious pixels or privacy. Forget cumbersome online tools; GetCompress brings blazing-fast, secure batch compression right to your machine, ensuring your data stays yours.

Image 2: GetCompress Review

Uniqueness 74%

The uniqueness score is 74%.

Utility 77%

The utility score is 77%.

Innovation 70% [...] Unrivaled Format Support: Compress and convert over 107 distinct formats, including HEIC, WebP, JPEG, PNG, AVIF, PSD, MP4, MOV, RAW ProRes, WebM, GIF, and PDF. One app to rule them all.
   Blazing Fast & Batch-Oriented: Drastically reduce file sizes by up to 90% with minimal quality loss, processing hundreds of files simultaneously.
   Offline & Secure by Design: All compression occurs locally on your device. No cloud uploads, no internet connection required, safeguarding your confidential files.
   Automated Workflow Integration: Streamline your routine with clipboard compression, folder monitoring, and macOS Finder context menu integration. [...] ## Main Target

GetCompress isn’t merely a utility; it’s an essential weapon in the arsenal of developers, creators, and power users. If you’re a web developer optimizing assets, a content producer managing vast media libraries, or anyone seeking to automate tedious compression tasks, GetCompress is your solution. Its deep integration capabilities, like MCP server connectivity for AI assistants and open-source extensions, empower you to weave local compression directly into your automated workflows. It’s the ultimate, lifetime-licensed desktop companion for streamlining media management and reclaiming both storage and sanity.

Have an AI Tool? Get Reviewed. Submit your tool and we publish a full review!

Submit Your Tool →

Image 37: Omri Ben-Shoham

Omri Ben-Shoham

The context-switching pain is real - I'm always mid-workflow when I need to compress something and end up in some janky browser tool or hunting for a half-remembered app. Having this as a lightweight desktop app that stays offline makes a lot of sense to me. Quick question: does it handle RAW photo files? That's usually where most compression tools fall apart for me.

I definitely relate to the "just let me drop a file, compress it, and move on" workflow. It's one of those tasks that should take seconds but somehow ends up breaking your flow.

One thing I'm curious about: among your early users, what file type surprised you the most in terms of compression results? Was there a format where people consistently said, "I didn't expect it to shrink that much without noticeable quality loss"?

Also, the MCP integration is a nice touch. Feels like a natural fit as more AI workflows start handling media.

Wishing you a fantastic launch!

results.title: GetCompress - PDF & Image - Apps on Google Play
results.content:
GetCompress is a fast, private PDF compressor and image compressor for reducing file size directly on your device.  
  
Compress PDF files, JPG images, PNG images, HEIC photos, and WebP files without uploads, cloud processing, accounts, or tracking. Your documents, photos, scans, receipts, forms, and images stay on your phone.  
  
Use GetCompress to compress PDFs, shrink images, reduce photo size, optimize documents, make files smaller, and prepare attachments for email, messaging apps, online forms, file uploads, portals, storage, and sharing. Everything runs locally on-device, so sensitive files never leave your phone. [...] Updated on

GetCompress is built for everyday file compression, PDF optimization, image size reduction, document sharing, storage cleanup, and secure offline compression.

• Image compressor for JPG, PNG, HEIC, and WebP  
• Compress PDF files directly on your device  
• Compress photos and images without uploading  
• Offline file compression with no internet required

results.title: GetCompress ∙ GetCompress for designers: client-ready exports without the bloat
results.content:
# Image Compression for Designers

How designers use GetCompress to shrink PNG, JPEG, WebP, HEIC, SVG, TIFF, and image-heavy PDF exports locally for fast handoffs to clients, product teams, and stakeholders.

TL;DR

Shrink PNG, JPEG, WebP, HEIC, SVG, TIFF, and image-heavy PDF exports on your Mac before handoff. Files load faster in review links and shared folders, and confidential work never touches an online compressor.

You finished the mockups. Then the email bounced because the attachment was too large. Or a PM opened the shared folder on mobile and waited thirty seconds for one PNG to load. Or the case-study PDF looked fine on your screen but crawled on a stakeholder’s laptop. [...] Design exports are heavy by default. GetCompress is a lightweight Mac app that compresses images, PDFs, videos, and GIFs locally, so handoffs to clients, product teams, and eng stay sharp without the wait.

## Why design exports exceed sharing limits

The problem is usually file size, not quality. A folder of retina PNG exports, a presentation PDF, or a screen-recording walkthrough can blow past email limits and slow every review link in Slack, Notion, or a shared drive.

Compress before handoff and file size is often reduced by 50-90%. Larger, higher-resolution exports usually benefit the most, so a full mockup set or scan-heavy deck can drop dramatically with little visible change.

## Image, PDF, and video formats in design handoffs
